html:
Dong Nai Port JSC (HOSE:PDN)

PDN is no longer the hidden 2024 bargain, but it is still a strong forward-looking compounding thesis. At the latest verified close of VND105,000, the stock trades around 13x trailing EPS, 12x my 2026 earnings estimate, and about 8x EV/EBITDA, with a 3.8% cash dividend yield and almost no institutional sponsorship. That is not statistically dirt cheap, but it is still too low for a 25%+ ROE port/logistics bottleneck sitting inside Vietnam’s fastest industrial-airport corridor.

The old market label is “small provincial state port.” The better label is “Dong Nai manufacturing toll road.” PDN operates Long Binh Tan and Go Dau, connecting inland industrial cargo with Cai Mep and HCMC port systems. Its 2025 annual report says PDN ranked top 8 in Vietnam container handling and top 13 in total port throughput, while 2025 revenue rose 17.8% and profit after tax rose 25.7% to VND436.5 billion. Container and logistics revenue reached VND1.061 trillion on 1.53 million TEU, while general cargo revenue reached VND515 billion on 7.65 million tons.

The 2026 evidence is still positive. Q1 revenue rose 6%, container volume rose 9.1%, and PAT reached VND110.5 billion, already 24.2% of the full-year profit plan. Management’s 2026 AGM target is VND1.67 trillion revenue and VND456 billion PAT, plus VND158 billion of capex and a VND4,000/share dividend. I think the plan is conservative: Q1 annualized against normal seasonality points closer to VND480-500 billion PAT if exports do not roll over.

The key upside torque is the B6/fuel-logistics transition at Go Dau. PDN’s annual report says it plans a 30,000 DWT B6 berth to receive petroleum products for Long Thanh Airport, and the AGM update said the fuel storage project was expected to start construction in May 2026 with two phases totaling about 1 million tons/year. This matters because it changes PDN from a pure container/general cargo handler into a specialized airport-fuel and logistics infrastructure platform. Long Thanh Phase 1 is targeted for 2026 commercial operation, and Dong Nai’s own planning around the airport is increasingly framed around logistics, distribution, free-trade, and industrial services.

The broader demand setup is also favorable. Vietnam’s Q1 2026 exports were up 19.1%, manufacturing value-added was strong, and electronics/machinery are now driving a larger share of trade. That is exactly the cargo base Dong Nai wants. On May 18, 2026, Dong Nai formally became a centrally administered city, and local policy commentary explicitly names ports, industrial parks, water, and environmental services as strategic state-linked infrastructure sectors. For PDN, state control is a discount, but it also means the company sits inside the official development plan rather than outside it.

Valuation is more nuanced than the prior thesis suggested. PDN is not trading at 2-4x EBITDA anymore, and the cash pile is not half the market cap. Simplize shows market cap around VND5.83 trillion, P/E 13.0x, P/B 3.9x, and EV/EBITDA 8.1x. Q1 filings show bank deposits over VND814 billion, but liabilities also rose to nearly VND496 billion, including short-term borrowings and dividends payable. The cash supports capex and dividends, but it is not the main reason to own the stock. The main reason is that 2026-2030 earnings power can compound faster than the market’s “provincial port” label implies.

Retail context supports the ignored-name angle. PDN trades tiny daily volume, had only 1,700 shares traded on the latest quoted day, and foreign ownership is effectively locked out by the 0% foreign-room limit cited in the annual report. Simply Wall St shows no community narratives. This is not a crowded Vietnam infrastructure trade. It is a domestic, illiquid, state-linked compounder that most global capital cannot touch.

The bear case is straightforward: export demand slows, Middle East disruption and tariff uncertainty squeeze customers, Long Thanh timing slips again, and PDN spends heavily on lower-return infrastructure. The stock also deserves a liquidity and governance discount. State control can mean disciplined long-term infrastructure ownership, but it can also mean minority shareholders wait while capital is recycled into public-policy projects.

The base case is that PDN earns VND490 billion in 2026, VND530-560 billion by 2027/28, keeps paying VND4,000+ per share, and the market gradually values it as a scarce high-ROE logistics infrastructure asset rather than a sleepy port. A 15-16x multiple on VND9,500-10,000 EPS supports VND145,000-160,000 without heroic assumptions.

The bull case is that B6 and Long Thanh fuel logistics become a real second engine, container growth continues at high single digits, margins hold, and 2030 management ambition of VND720 billion pretax profit proves beatable. At VND600-700 billion PAT and an 18x multiple for a scarce airport-linked infrastructure platform, PDN can be a VND210,000-240,000 stock.

Targets from VND105,000 current price: Bear: VND78,000, down 26%. Base: VND150,000, up 43%. Bull: VND230,000, up 119%.
